items.title: Trump warns 'clock is ticking' for Iran as peace progress stalls
items.summary: On Monday Iran said it had responded to the latest US proposal and that exchanges with Washington were continuing.

items.title: Bodies of missing Italian divers found in Maldives
items.summary: The bodies of all five Italian divers who died in the Maldives last week have now been found, officials tell the BBC.

items.title: At least six Americans exposed to Ebola during DR Congo outbreak
items.summary: One of the six Americans believed to have been exposed is experiencing symptoms, according to media reports.

items.title: Israeli forces boarding Gaza-bound flotilla near Cyprus, activists say
items.summary: Live broadcasts from the Global Sumud flotilla show commandos boarding several boats trying to breach the Gaza maritime blockade.

items.title: Strike over high fuel prices paralyses transport in Kenya
items.summary: Key roads have remained largely empty and some schools have asked students to stay at home.

items.title: Driver of crashed train tested positive for drugs, Thai police say
items.summary: The freight train collided with a bus in Bangkok, killing eight people and injuring dozens of others.

items.title: How worrying is the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo?
items.summary: An outbreak in DR Congo has been declared a health emergency, with almost 250 suspected cases reported.

items.title: 'This may be the last time you hear my voice': Political executions surge in Iran since start of war
items.summary: Since the US and Israel attacked Iran on  28 February, the UN says it’s verified the execution of at least 32 political prisoners.

items.title: Moment two fighter jets collide mid-air at US air show
items.summary: Four crew members safely ejected from US Navy jets and are in a stable condition after the collision.

items.title: Large-scale Ukrainian drone attack kills three in Moscow region, says Russia
items.summary: Ukraine's president says the strikes are a "justified" response to deadly Russian attacks on Ukrainian cities.

items.title: Escaped tiger shot by German police after attacking man
items.summary: The tiger escaped a facility owned by local trainer Carmen Zander, also known as Germany's "Tiger Queen", according to local media reports.

items.title: UAE reports strike near Abu Dhabi nuclear power plant
items.summary: The country is investigating the source of the strike, which it calls a "dangerous escalation".

items.title: 'Everest Man' and 'Mountain Queen' break own records scaling world's tallest peak
items.summary: Kami Rita Sherpa and Lakpa Sherpa summited Everest for the 32nd and 11th time, respectively.

items.title: Taiwan will not provoke conflict nor give up sovereignty, says president
items.summary: Lai Ching-te made his first direct response to Trump and Xi's meeting where they discussed Taiwan independence.

items.title: Man drives car into pedestrians in Italy, injuring eight
items.summary: Eight people were injured, four seriously, before passers-by gave chase and stopped the man.

items.title: Hezbollah drone strike videos show evolving tactics against Israel
items.summary: BBC Verify has examined dozens of videos of fibre-optic drone attacks carried out by the Lebanese armed group.

items.title: Taiwan insists it is independent after Trump warning
items.summary: Fresh from his summit in Beijing, the US president warned against a formal declaration of independence by the island.

items.title: Trump-backed challenger defeats Republican senator who voted to convict president
items.summary: Trump threw his full support behind Julia Letlow in Louisiana's high-stakes contest, branding the incumbent "disloyal".

items.title: Canadian from hantavirus-hit cruise ship tests positive
items.summary: The individual is one of four former passengers on the MV Hondius isolating on Vancouver Island, British Columbia.

items.title: Rescue diver dies during search for bodies of Italians who drowned in Maldives caves
items.summary: Staff Sgt Mohamed Mahdhee died while diving in the search-and-rescue operation, the Maldives government tells the BBC.

items.title: Toddlers among more than 50 schoolchildren kidnapped in Nigeria
items.summary: No group has yet claimed responsibility for the attacks, which have devastated Mussa town in Borno state.

items.title: Inside the 'kill-zone' on Ukraine's front line, where new weapons have transformed war
items.summary: Machines are increasingly replacing humans on the front line, but troops are still vital for defending territory.

items.title: From escaping child marriage 'to an old pervert' to becoming Sierra Leone's first lady
items.summary: The private world of one of Africa's most powerful political women is revealed in a BBC World Service exclusive.

items.title: Foreign tourists are falling out of love with Goa - here's why
items.summary: Foreign visitors to Goa have nearly halved from their pre-Covid peak even as domestic tourism is booming.

items.title: Secret police and propaganda: The Chinese agents spying on expats in US
items.summary: Experts say two espionage cases in the US highlight China's attempts to peddle influence globally over the past decade.

items.title: 'Look Mum, one point': Why does the UK keep getting Eurovision wrong?
items.summary: After four years of flops, the BBC has to think hard about how it approaches Eurovision in 2027.

items.title: The girl group who've sold out venues and toured the world - without releasing a record
items.summary: The all-female supergroup has sold out venues across the UK and Ireland, toured stadiums with Ed Sheeran, and built a global following - all without releasing a debut record.

items.title: Eurovision winner Dara arrives to screaming fans in Bulgaria
items.summary: The 27-year-old's tune Bangaranga won Bulgaria its first ever title in the song contest.

items.title: Lights, camera, Bangaranga: Highlights from Eurovision
items.summary: Bulgarian singer Dara stormed to victory in the contest, while Ukraine broke the record for the longest note ever sung at Eurovision.

items.title: Watch: What did we learn from Trump's visit to China?
items.summary: President Trump has concluded a two-day visit to Beijing at a high-stakes summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

items.title: BBC on the ground during march through Jerusalem's Old City
items.summary: Tens of thousands of Israeli celebrants have marched through the area captured by Israel in the 1967 Arab-Israeli war.

items.title: Hundreds of illegal motorbikes bulldozed in New York City crime crackdown
items.summary: A moped had been used in the deadly stray-bullet shooting of a 7-month-old in Brooklyn last month, officials said.
